gambit.description: State something the target knows is true but doesn't want to hear. Target gains [Shaken].

exploit.name: Pivot
exploit.description: If a social exchange turns against Varth, he immediately redirects the conversation to a new topic. Resets social conditions on himself.

threatBuild.notes: Varth is the campaign's central NPC — charming, cynical, self-serving. Power 5 = (Pres 5-1)+2. Defense 2 = max(1,2)-1+2. Resist 4 = Grit 3-1+2+1(disciplined). He is VERY hard to read (Resist 4) and VERY good at reading others. In combat, he hides behind the party.

scenes.hazards: Detention block — automated defenses, elite guards. Time pressure from Inquisitor's arrival.

decisions.consequence: Fastest exit but Varth refuses to cooperate without his cylinders. May need to drag him.
Takes extra time and risk but secures Varth's full cooperation and the Project Leviathan data.

narrativeLinks.note: Varth immediately begins directing the heroes toward Darga the Hutt.

encounters.name: Meeting Varth
encounters.trigger: Heroes breach the detention block
encounters.description: Varth is not a broken prisoner — he's been waiting. He's already packed. The guard is confused and half-charmed. Varth treats this like a business meeting, not a rescue.
encounters.tactics: Varth controls the conversation. He assesses the heroes immediately and decides how to handle each one. He's testing them.

encounters.name: The Cylinder Retrieval
encounters.trigger: Varth insists on retrieving his data cylinders from Skarn's vault
encounters.description: Side mission under time pressure. The vault is on the second floor, east wing. Two guards, an electronic lock, and Skarn's paranoia.
encounters.tactics: Speed vs. thoroughness. The Draco clock is still running. Every minute spent here is a minute less for escape.

skillChecks.context: Reading Varth — is he an ally, a liability, or something worse?
skillChecks.success: Varth is charming because it's a survival strategy. He's genuinely brilliant, genuinely selfish, and genuinely dangerous. He will betray anyone if the math works out.
skillChecks.failure: He seems like a charismatic, slightly eccentric officer who got in over his head.

skillChecks.context: Convincing Varth to leave WITHOUT the cylinders.
skillChecks.success: Varth reluctantly agrees — but makes it clear this will cost them later. He holds it over the heroes.
skillChecks.failure: Varth flatly refuses. 'Without those cylinders, I'm just a dead admiral. With them, I'm rich. We're getting the cylinders.'

skillChecks.context: Building rapport with Varth during the initial meeting.
skillChecks.success: Varth warms to the heroes. Gives them a genuine piece of advice about Draco: 'He doesn't negotiate. He collects.'
skillChecks.failure: Varth is polite but guarded. Treats the heroes as hired help.

skillChecks.context: Cracking Skarn's vault to retrieve the data cylinders.
skillChecks.success: Clean entry. Cylinders secured. No additional alarms.
skillChecks.failure: The vault alarm triggers. Guards converge on the east wing.
skillChecks.isGated: Only if heroes agree to retrieve Varth's cylinders

skillChecks.context: Examining the cylinders while Varth isn't watching.
skillChecks.success: The cylinders contain financial records — massive sums routed through shell accounts. 'Project Leviathan' appears repeatedly.
skillChecks.failure: The cylinders are heavily encrypted. Can't read them without specialized equipment.

environmentMechanics.name: Draco Clock (Continued)
environmentMechanics.effect: The clock keeps ticking. Every detour — including the cylinder retrieval — eats into escape time.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Move fast. Make hard choices about what's worth the time.

environmentMechanics.name: Detention Block Security
environmentMechanics.effect: Heavier guard presence than other fortress sections. Automated cell locks. Surveillance cameras.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Generator sabotage (if completed in Scene 8) disables most of this.

environmentMechanics.name: Varth's Cooperation
environmentMechanics.effect: Varth fully cooperates and shares intel only if his cylinders are secured. Without them, he's sulky, uncooperative, and may try to escape on his own later.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Get the cylinders. Or be very persuasive.

intel: Varth embezzled from Project Leviathan
The codes to his fortune are held by his former partner — Darga the Hutt
Project Leviathan is described as a 'mobile siege platform in the Unknown Regions'
Varth's warning about Draco: 'He doesn't negotiate. He collects.'
connections: Admiral Gilder Varth (the campaign's central NPC — charming, unreliable, essential)

pacing.openingBeat: Blow the door expecting a broken man. Find Varth gambling with his guard.
pacing.risingAction: Varth's demands. The cylinder retrieval. Time pressure mounting.
pacing.climax: The choice: get the cylinders and risk Draco, or drag Varth out now.
pacing.resolution: Varth is secured. The heroes have what they came for — maybe more.

scenes.title: The Deal Gone Bad
scenes.subtitle: Climax
scenes.readAloud:
The landing pad erupts. The Stormtroopers open fire on Skarn's mercenaries. The fortress becomes a three-way warzone — Imperials, the Warlord's forces, and you caught in the middle.

Then a shadow falls over the courtyard. A shuttle screams overhead, engines howling, and you see the crimson markings of the Inquisitorius. Draco has arrived.

Mandrake grabs your arm. "Run. Into the jungle. NOW."
scenes.gmNotes: Three-way firefight: Imperials vs. Warlord vs. Heroes. Draco's shuttle deploys Shadow Troopers to cut off retreat. Captain Okeefe swoops in with the Banshee (or the heroes' repaired ship), strafing Imperial positions to buy time. The heroes blast off as Imperial walkers breach the treeline. In hyperspace, Varth reveals Project Leviathan is a massive mobile siege platform in the Unknown Regions, and the codes to his fortune are held by his former partner — Darga the Hutt. This sets up Adventure 2.

npcs.name: Inquisitor Valin Draco
npcs.notes: Arrives via shuttle. The campaign's recurring villain. Does not appear in person in this scene — glimpsed from distance.
npcs.behavior: Draco does not engage the heroes directly in this scene. He arrives, deploys his Shadow Troopers, and begins methodically securing the fortress. The heroes see him from a distance — a dark figure descending from the shuttle ramp. This is a preview, not a confrontation.
npcs.intel: Draco is an Inquisitor — a Force-sensitive Imperial operative. He is here specifically for Varth. The heroes will face him directly in later adventures.

passive.name: Dark Side Presence
passive.description: All enemies within close range must resist or gain [Rattled] when Draco enters the scene. Force-sensitive characters feel nausea and dread.

actions.name: Lightsaber Strike
actions.description: Melee attack with lightsaber. +1 Power. Exploding Power die.

actions.name: Force Crush
actions.description: Ranged Force attack. Targets Endure only. Ignores cover.

actions.name: Mind Probe
actions.description: Force-assisted interrogation. Social attack bypassing [Guarded]. Targets Resist.

maneuver.name: Force Pull
maneuver.description: Move one target or object one range band closer. No resistance.

gambit.name: Force Choke
gambit.description: Target gains [Restrained] and [Hazard 2] on hit. Maintained until Draco acts again.

exploit.name: Inevitable
exploit.description: When Draco would be reduced to 0 Vitality, he instead drops to 1 Vitality and gains [Buffered 3]. Once per encounter.

threatBuild.notes: NEMESIS. Draco does NOT fight the party in Adv1 — he arrives, demonstrates power, and leaves. This stat block is for the campaign. Power 8 = (Grit 5-1)+4. Defense 7 = max(3,3)-1+4+1. Evasion 5 = 3-1+4-1(mystic). Resist 8 = 5-1+4. Vitality 16 = (3+5+4)*2(boss). 2 actions/round. Boss: N-1 Exploits. He is designed to be unbeatable at this point in the campaign.

npcs.name: Shadow Troopers
npcs.notes: Black-armored elite commandos. Stealth suits. Cutting off retreat.
npcs.behavior: Deployed to the jungle perimeter. They move through the trees like ghosts. Their job is to prevent anyone leaving the fortress alive. They are significantly more dangerous than standard Stormtroopers.
npcs.intel: Shadow Troopers are Inquisitorius assets — black ops. Stealth-equipped, enhanced training, independent targeting.

passive.name: Stealth Field
passive.description: Shadow Troopers are invisible to normal sight while stationary. Attacking breaks concealment for 1 round. Sensor-based detection (Tech check) is required to spot them.

actions.name: Precision Strike
actions.description: Ranged attack from concealment. +1 Power if target is unaware.

actions.name: Vibroblade
actions.description: Silent melee attack. Does not break team concealment.

maneuver.name: Reactivate Cloak
maneuver.description: If in cover, re-enter stealth. Requires 1 round without attacking.

gambit.name: Flash-Bang
gambit.description: Area effect. All targets in zone gain [Blinded] for 1 round.

exploit.name: Coordinated Ambush
exploit.description: If all Shadow Troopers attack the same target in the same round, +2 Power on each attack.

threatBuild.notes: Elite Imperial commandos. Power 4 = (Ref 4-1)+2. Defense 5 = max(2,4)-1+2+2(medium armor). Evasion 4 = 4-1+2-1(medium armor). Resist 4 = Grit 3-1+2. Vitality 7 = 2+3+2. These are the real threat in the finale — the party should feel outmatched.

npcs.name: Captain Okeefe
npcs.notes: Provides air support and extraction.
npcs.behavior: Okeefe brings the Banshee in hot, strafing Imperial positions to buy the heroes time. She's risking everything — this is beyond her contract.
dialogue: "I see you! Get to the clearing — I'm making one pass!"
"That's an Inquisitor shuttle. You didn't mention an Inquisitor. My fee just tripled."

passive.name: Veteran Smuggler
passive.description: Piloting checks in the Banshee gain +1 Power. Immune to [Rattled] from Imperial threats.

actions.name: Strafing Run
actions.description: Ship-scale attack run. Area damage in one zone.

actions.name: Covering Fire
actions.description: Suppressive fire from the Banshee's turrets. Targets gain [Suppressed].

maneuver.name: Low Pass
maneuver.description: Fly the Banshee overhead. Allies in the zone can board as a maneuver.

gambit.name: Hard Burn
gambit.description: Push engines. +2 zones movement but ship takes 1 system stress.

exploit.name: Extraction
exploit.description: Land the Banshee under fire. Allies can board. Ship takes hits during the maneuver.

threatBuild.notes: Same NPC as earlier scenes. In the finale she is in the cockpit providing air support and extraction. Power 5, Defense 5. She is an ally — the GM runs her to support the party escape.

scenes.hazards: Three-way firefight. Imperial walkers approaching. Shadow Troopers flanking. Time-critical extraction.

decisions.choice: Fight through to the landing pad
decisions.consequence: Direct but dangerous — heavy Imperial presence.

decisions.choice: Flee into the jungle for Okeefe's pickup
decisions.consequence: Must evade Shadow Troopers in dense canopy. Okeefe provides strafing support.

narrativeLinks.note: Varth's reveal about Darga holding the encryption keys launches Adventure 2.

encounters.name: Three-Way Firefight
encounters.trigger: The landing pad erupts as Imperials and Skarn's forces clash
encounters.description: The fortress becomes a warzone. Three factions: Imperials securing the perimeter, Skarn's mercenaries defending their territory, and the heroes trying to escape through the middle.
encounters.tactics: Use the chaos. The Imperials and mercs are fighting each other — the heroes can exploit the confusion to slip through. But Shadow Troopers are specifically hunting for anyone trying to leave.

composition.positioning: Imperials and mercs fighting each other; heroes threading through

encounters.name: Shadow Trooper Ambush
encounters.trigger: When the heroes reach the jungle perimeter
encounters.description: Four Shadow Troopers emerge from concealment in the treeline. Black armor, stealth suits, precision fire. They are the real obstacle to escape.
encounters.tactics: Shadow Troopers fight smart — overlapping fields of fire, tactical movement, targeting the weakest member. They don't charge; they control the engagement distance.

composition.positioning: Concealed in canopy, overlapping fields of fire

encounters.name: Okeefe's Extraction
encounters.trigger: After clearing or bypassing the Shadow Troopers
encounters.description: Okeefe swoops in with the Banshee, strafing Imperial positions. The heroes must reach the clearing and board under fire. Varth provides coordinates for the hyperspace jump.
encounters.tactics: Running to the ship while Okeefe covers them. Someone needs to man the dorsal turret. The ship takes hits during liftoff.

skillChecks.context: Reading the three-way battle to find the safest path through.
skillChecks.success: A gap in the fighting — the heroes can move through with minimal contact.
skillChecks.failure: No clear path. They have to fight through at least one engagement.

skillChecks.context: Dodging through crossfire between Imperial and mercenary positions.
skillChecks.success: Clean movement through the kill zone. No hits taken.
skillChecks.failure: Caught in crossfire. Ranged damage from stray shots.

skillChecks.context: Engaging the Shadow Troopers in the treeline.
skillChecks.success: Precise fire drops or suppresses them. Path to the clearing opens.
skillChecks.failure: Shadow Troopers return devastating fire from cover. Heroes are pinned.

skillChecks.context: Sprint to Okeefe's ship across open ground.
skillChecks.success: Full speed. Board the ship. Go.
skillChecks.failure: Stumble, exhaustion, or carrying wounded slows the run. More time under fire.

skillChecks.context: Liftoff under fire. Breaking atmosphere with a Star Destroyer in orbit.
skillChecks.success: Clean escape. Jump to hyperspace before fighters can intercept.
skillChecks.failure: Ship takes heavy damage during liftoff. Emergency repairs needed in hyperspace.

environmentMechanics.name: Three-Way Crossfire
environmentMechanics.effect: Stray fire from all directions. Moving through contested areas without cover risks catching bolts from any faction.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Use Tactics to read the battle. Move during lulls. Use smoke grenades for concealment.

environmentMechanics.name: Shadow Trooper Stealth
environmentMechanics.effect: Shadow Troopers are nearly invisible in the treeline. First shot comes without warning.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Togruta echolocation, Rodian infrared, or Cathar hearing can detect them before visual contact. Investigation check to spot thermal shimmer.

environmentMechanics.name: Imperial Walker Approach
environmentMechanics.trigger: After 3-4 combat rounds in the fortress
environmentMechanics.effect: AT-DP walkers breach the treeline. Heavy weapons fire. The fortress is about to be overrun.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Leave NOW. The walkers make escape on foot nearly impossible.

environmentMechanics.name: Okeefe's Strafing Window
environmentMechanics.effect: Okeefe provides one strafing pass that suppresses Imperial positions for 2 rounds. After that, she has to pull up or get shot down.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Time the run to the clearing with Okeefe's pass.

intel: Project Leviathan is a massive mobile siege platform being built in the Unknown Regions
The encryption keys to Varth's fortune are held by Darga the Hutt — his former partner
Inquisitor Draco is now aware the heroes exist
Varth reveals the setup for Adventure 2: they must go to Darga to get the keys
connections: Captain Okeefe (loyalty proven — she risked everything)
Admiral Varth (the 'Cool Uncle' — charming, dangerous, essential)

pacing.openingBeat: The landing pad erupts. Stormtroopers vs. mercenaries. Draco's shuttle screams overhead.
pacing.risingAction: Running through a three-way firefight. Shadow Troopers in the treeline. Walkers approaching.
pacing.climax: The Shadow Trooper ambush at the perimeter. The hardest fight of the adventure.
pacing.resolution: Okeefe's extraction. Liftoff under fire. Hyperspace. Varth's reveal: Darga the Hutt holds the keys. Adventure 2 begins.

adventures.title: A Wretched Hive
adventures.summary: The heroes travel to Takodana to infiltrate Darga the Hutt's fortress, posing as entertainment to steal the encryption keys to Varth's fortune. They discover a captive Jedi Master and must choose between the mission and their conscience.

gearNotes.reason: Varth coaches the heroes over encrypted comms from outside Darga's palace. Open comlinks in a Hutt fortress means blown cover. Hard permission gate for the entire Long Con sequence.
The entire mission objective is slicing Darga's datapad to extract the encryption keys. Without a proper slicing interface, the core technical challenge is stepped down or impossible.
High-security detention block locks where Jedi Master Denia is held. Without one, bypassing these suffers heavy penalties.
The Tech's audition — ordered to fix Darga's hover-sled with a blaster at their head. No toolkit means stepped-down rolls on a pass/fail moment.
Multi-scene combat from the hit squad through the fortress escape. No medical facilities available.
Underworld social currency in Blackwind Point. Opens doors in criminal circles where credits draw suspicion. Varth's shopping list should include this.
Plant bugs near the Iron Ring delegation or Lady Sinde's private meetings. The intel from these events reveals Project Leviathan's slave labor connection.
Intercepting unencrypted palace communications — security patrol schedules, delivery timings, who talks to whom.
Recording evidence of slave trade and Imperial deals. Leverage material for later adventures.
Heavy combat droid at the filtration plant and elite security droids in the dungeon. Ion damage is the purpose-built counter.
Sealed blast doors during fortress lockdown. Overload Mode cuts through any door as a single action.
Covering movement through hostile corridors during lockdown and the hangar escape.
Quick terminal bypass in the dungeon to access stasis controls. Consumable, single action.
Hangar bay area denial against clustered Stormtroopers.
Multi-scene fighting with no rest. Someone is running on fumes by the hangar escape.

scenes.title: The Welcoming Committee
scenes.readAloud:
You disembark at Blackwind Point — a muddy, desperate shantytown on the far shore of Nymeve Lake. The air is thick with the smell of rotting vegetation and desperation. Refugees huddle in makeshift shelters while criminals eye your gear with open hunger.

The growl of repulsor-bikes cuts through the noise. A swoop gang circles your landing pad, engines revving. Their leader — a scarred human woman with a vibro-blade strapped to her leg — pulls up and kills her engine.

"Welcome to the other side of the lake. Landing tax is five hundred credits. Non-negotiable."
scenes.gmNotes: Kessra and The Vipers demand a landing tax. This sets the tone for Takodana's dark side: might makes right. If the heroes fight and win, locals regard them with respect and fear. If they pay, they're marked as easy targets. Kessra may reappear later as a hired guard in Darga's palace, holding a grudge. Varth has briefed the heroes: they need to find his contact Warrick Raden, a Devaronian fixer who can get them into Darga's court.

npcs.name: Kessra
npcs.notes: Scarred human woman. Leader of The Vipers. May reappear in Darga's fortress.

passive.name: Fleet Footed
passive.description: May take 2 maneuvers per turn instead of 1.

actions.name: Vibroblade Slash
actions.description: Melee attack with vibroblade. Fast and vicious.

actions.name: Drive-By Shot
actions.description: Ranged attack from a moving swoop. +1 zone movement after.

maneuver.name: Reposition
maneuver.description: Move without provoking reactions.

gambit.name: Hamstring
gambit.description: Target gains [Slowed] on hit.

exploit.name: Hit and Fade
exploit.description: After attacking, immediately mount swoop and move 2 zones.

threatBuild.notes: Kessra is a recurring NPC. Power 3 = (Ref 4-1)+1. May reappear as hired muscle in Darga's palace later.

npcs.name: The Vipers
npcs.notes: Armed with blasters and vibro-blades. Intimidation over combat skill.

passive.name: Fleet Footed
passive.description: May take 2 maneuvers per turn.

actions.name: Blaster Snap

actions.name: Vibroblade Lunge
actions.description: Melee attack.

maneuver.name: Reposition
maneuver.description: Move without provoking reactions.

gambit.name: Hamstring
gambit.description: Target gains [Slowed].

exploit.description: If leader is downed, all Vipers disengage and flee.

threatBuild.notes: Minion gang. Power 2 = (Ref 3-1)+0. Masterful+ = takedown. Paper tigers — flee if Kessra falls.

scenes.hazards: Open landing pad — surrounded by hostile gang. Muddy terrain. Refugees as potential collateral.

decisions.consequence: Marked as easy targets. Pickpockets and grifters will target you.
If successful, the Vipers back down. Local respect earned.
Vipers retreat if beaten. Newfound respect (and fear) from locals. Kessra holds a grudge.

skillChecks.context: Staring down Kessra and the Vipers to avoid paying landing tax.
skillChecks.success: Kessra reads you as dangerous. Backs off with a sneer. Locals take notice.
skillChecks.failure: Kessra doesn't flinch. The Vipers close in.

skillChecks.context: Talking down the situation without violence or payment.
skillChecks.success: Kessra laughs — she likes confidence. Waives the tax "just this once."
skillChecks.failure: Kessra sees through it. Price doubles.

skillChecks.context: Reading Kessra's intentions — shakedown or genuine threat?
skillChecks.success: Pure shakedown. The Vipers are a paper tiger — they won't fight someone who pushes back.
skillChecks.failure: Can't tell. She seems genuinely dangerous.

skillChecks.context: Scanning the landing pad area for tactical details.
skillChecks.success: Vipers positioned to flee, not fight. Their swoops are running — they want a quick exit if things go wrong.
skillChecks.failure: Chaotic scene. Too many variables.

encounters.name: Viper Shakedown
encounters.trigger: On arrival at Blackwind Point
encounters.description: Kessra and 6 Vipers demand 500 credits. Social challenge with combat fallback.
encounters.tactics: Kessra leads verbally. Vipers fan out to cut off exits. If fighting starts, they use swoops for hit-and-run.

environmentMechanics.name: Muddy Landing Pad
environmentMechanics.effect: Difficult terrain. Movement costs double. Swoops ignore this.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Stay on the ferrocrete pad edges.

environmentMechanics.name: Refugee Crowd
environmentMechanics.effect: Civilians scatter. Stray shots risk hitting bystanders.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Controlled fire only. Area attacks cause collateral.

rewards.narrative: Local reputation established — respected or marked as easy targets.

pacing.openingBeat: The Vipers circle the landing pad.
pacing.risingAction: Kessra makes her demand. Tension builds.
pacing.climax: The party's response — pay, talk, or fight.
pacing.resolution: The Vipers withdraw one way or another. The party enters Blackwind Point.

scenes.title: Hunting the Fixer
scenes.readAloud:
The encampment is a maze of tents, grounded starships, and mud-brick hovels. Every face you pass wears the same expression — guarded suspicion.

Through bribes, threats, and persistence, a pattern emerges: Warrick Raden has gone to ground. The nervous Devaronian used to run errands for Darga but vanished from the cantinas recently. Word is he skimmed money from the Hutt and is hiding out at the old Water Filtration Plant on the edge of the swamp.
scenes.gmNotes: A social/investigation scene. The heroes must navigate the underworld of Blackwind Point to find Raden. Local bartenders, refugees, and fences can provide information. The Water Filtration Plant is a rusted industrial facility half-sunk in the bog. This is a good opportunity for roleplaying and gathering intel about Darga's current state — he's become paranoid and obsessed with his new Imperial guests.

npcs.name: Local Contacts
npcs.notes: Bartenders, refugees, fences who can point to Raden's location.

passive.name: Street Smart
passive.description: Knows the local power structure and who to avoid.

actions.name: Tip Off
actions.description: Provide information about a local person or place.

actions.name: Warn Away
actions.description: Social pressure to make strangers leave.

maneuver.name: Gossip Network
maneuver.description: Spread or gather rumors.

threatBuild.notes: Non-combatant NPCs. Social interaction targets only. Represent bartenders, fences, refugees.

scenes.hazards: Pickpockets, grifters, and informants who might tip off Darga's network.

decisions.consequence: Fastest method. Costs credits but gets reliable intel.
Free but may attract attention from Darga's informants.

skillChecks.context: Gathering intel on Raden's location from the encampment.
skillChecks.success: Multiple sources confirm: Water Filtration Plant, edge of the swamp. He's been hiding there 3 days.
skillChecks.failure: Conflicting information. Two locations — one is a trap.

skillChecks.context: Loosening tongues at the cantinas. Buying drinks, making friends.
skillChecks.success: A bartender opens up. Raden was in yesterday, jumpy. Mentioned "the plant."
skillChecks.failure: Everyone clams up. Strangers asking about Raden draws suspicion.

skillChecks.context: Posing as someone Raden owes money to, or as Darga's people.
skillChecks.success: A fence points you to the filtration plant, hoping to collect a finder's fee.
skillChecks.failure: The fence reports your inquiry to Darga's network. Hit squad arrives faster.

skillChecks.context: Avoiding Darga's informants while asking questions.
skillChecks.success: Your inquiries stay below the radar. No alert to Darga.
skillChecks.failure: Someone tips off Darga. The hit squad in Scene 4 arrives 1 round earlier.

skillChecks.context: Reading the encampment's social dynamics. Who runs what.
skillChecks.success: Clear picture: Darga's reach extends through the cantinas but not the refugee camp. The refugees are an information blind spot.
skillChecks.failure: Everyone seems equally untrustworthy.

environmentMechanics.name: Informant Network
environmentMechanics.effect: Darga has eyes in Blackwind Point. Aggressive questioning or mentions of "Varth" trigger alerts.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Use cover identities. Ask about Raden obliquely.

environmentMechanics.name: Refugee Camp Buffer
environmentMechanics.trigger: If heroes investigate the refugee area
environmentMechanics.effect: Refugees distrust everyone equally — harder to get info but also harder for Darga's network to track inquiries here.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Spend time. Build trust. Medicine or supply gifts open doors.

rewards.narrative: Location of Warrick Raden confirmed.

pacing.openingBeat: The encampment is a maze. Every face is guarded.
pacing.risingAction: Leads converge on the Water Filtration Plant.
pacing.climax: The heroes decide how to approach the plant.
pacing.resolution: Raden's location is confirmed. The party moves out.

scenes.title: The Rat in the Trap
scenes.readAloud:
The filtration plant is a noisy, steam-venting structure half-sunk in the bog. You find Warrick Raden hiding in a maintenance shed — a nervous wreck clutching a blaster and jumping at shadows.

When you mention Varth's name, Raden's face cycles through shock, fear, and desperate hope in rapid succession. "Varth? He's alive? Stars help me — he's alive."
scenes.gmNotes: Raden thought Varth was dead. He confirms he can get the heroes into Darga's palace but refuses to go back freely. He reveals that Darga has 'changed' — becoming paranoid and obsessed with Imperial guests. The heroes must de-escalate Raden's panic and convince him to cooperate. He's their ticket into the palace.

npcs.name: Warrick Raden
npcs.notes: Nervous wreck. Owes Varth a debt. The heroes' entry ticket to Darga's palace.

passive.name: Fixer's Contacts
passive.description: Raden knows everyone in Darga's operation. Can identify guards, routines, and weak points.

actions.name: Spill Info
actions.description: Provide palace intel when motivated.

actions.name: Panic Shot
actions.description: Wild blaster shot when cornered. Low accuracy.

maneuver.name: Bolt
maneuver.description: Attempt to flee. Must be intercepted with Athletics or Evasion.

threatBuild.notes: Raden is not a fighter. Power 3 = (Wits 4-1)+0. His value is information, not combat.

scenes.hazards: Steam pipes, unstable industrial equipment. Raden is armed and panicky.

decisions.consequence: He agrees to help if the heroes protect him from Darga's retribution.
He cooperates but may betray the heroes at a critical moment.

skillChecks.context: Calming Raden down. He's panicking with a blaster aimed at you.
skillChecks.success: Raden lowers the blaster. Starts talking. Relieved that Varth is alive.
skillChecks.failure: Raden stays panicky. Blaster stays up. Additional checks needed to de-escalate.

skillChecks.context: Convincing Raden to help get the heroes into Darga's palace.
skillChecks.success: Raden agrees if the heroes promise to protect him from Darga.
skillChecks.failure: Raden wants more — credits, guaranteed extraction, or Darga dead.

skillChecks.context: Threatening Raden into compliance.
skillChecks.success: Raden caves immediately — he's a wreck. But he'll look for ways to betray you later.
skillChecks.failure: Raden's panic overrides his fear. He bolts or shoots wildly.

skillChecks.context: Reading Raden. Is he trustworthy?
skillChecks.success: Genuinely terrified. He owes Varth a real debt. He'll cooperate as long as he believes you can protect him.
skillChecks.failure: Hard to read through the panic. Could be genuine or could be stalling.

skillChecks.context: Searching the maintenance shed for intel.
skillChecks.success: Raden's notes: sketched palace maps, guard shift times, Darga's paranoid habits. Useful intelligence.
skillChecks.failure: Just a panicking man's hideout. Nothing organized.

environmentMechanics.name: Steam-Filled Shed
environmentMechanics.effect: Hissing pipes, condensation, poor visibility at distance. Concealment for anyone more than 1 zone away.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Stay close. Voice carries — social checks are unaffected.

environmentMechanics.name: Armed Panicker
environmentMechanics.effect: Raden has a blaster and twitchy trigger finger. First social failure may result in him firing wildly.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: De-escalate before approaching. Slow movements.

items.name: Palace Maps (sketch)

rewards.narrative: Raden agrees to get the heroes into Darga's court.

pacing.openingBeat: Raden has a blaster aimed at the door.
pacing.risingAction: Negotiation — what does Raden need to cooperate?
pacing.climax: The deal is struck or forced.
pacing.resolution: Raden commits to the palace infiltration plan.

scenes.title: The Hit Squad
scenes.readAloud:
The negotiation is interrupted by the sound of heavy boots on metal. Through the grimy windows, you see movement — Trandoshan Mercenaries, heavily armed, surrounding the filtration plant. A heavy combat droid stomps into position at the main entrance.

Darga has grown tired of waiting for Raden to come home.
scenes.gmNotes: Darga sent a hit squad to silence Raden. Trandoshan Mercenaries and a heavy combat droid besiege the shed. The fight takes place in cramped machinery — steam pipes burst, obscuring vision. The Trandoshans use the environment to hunt. The heroes MUST protect Raden — if he dies, their easy route into the palace dies with him.

npcs.name: Trandoshan Mercenaries
npcs.notes: Hired guns. Using the industrial environment to hunt.

passive.name: Unrelenting

actions.name: Hunting Rifle
actions.description: Ranged attack at medium range.

npcs.name: Heavy Combat Droid
npcs.notes: Blocking the main entrance. Heavy armor and weapons.

passive.name: Unrelenting
passive.description: Ignore the first [Weakened] condition per scene.

Must punch through the Trandoshans to reach an exit. Raden is vulnerable during movement.

skillChecks.success: They're coming from three directions — rear maintenance hatch is unguarded.
skillChecks.failure: No clear picture. React to contact.

skillChecks.success: Steam erupts in a wall of scalding vapor. Trandoshans are forced back 2 rounds.
skillChecks.failure: Machinery sputters. Partial obstruction only.

skillChecks.success: Catwalk falls. Two Trandoshans are pinned under wreckage.
skillChecks.failure: Catwalk buckles but holds. Creates difficult terrain only.

skillChecks.failure: Raden is alive but badly hurt. Must be carried.

encounters.tactics: Trandoshans use scent-tracking in the steam. The droid advances slowly, absorbing fire. Protect Raden.

pacing.openingBeat: Heavy boots on metal. Shadows in the windows.
pacing.risingAction: Trandoshans breach from multiple points. Steam and chaos.
pacing.climax: The heavy combat droid advances. Must be dealt with to escape.
pacing.resolution: Hit squad is down. Raden is shaken but alive.

scenes.title: The Proposition
scenes.readAloud:
With the hit squad defeated, Raden is terrified but alive. He realizes he can never stay in the encampment. His eyes dart to your weapons, to the door, and finally settle on acceptance.

"Alright. I know how to get you in. Darga is hosting a delegation of high rollers and business partners. He wants entertainment — gladiators, gamblers, mercenaries. I'll present you to the Hutt as 'prime talent.' But once we're inside..." He swallows hard. "...you're on your own."
scenes.gmNotes: Raden proposes presenting the heroes as entertainment for Darga's court. Options: gladiators for his arena, high-stakes gamblers, or specialized mercenaries. He warns that Darga is hosting Imperial Mining Guild members — heroes must hide political affiliations. The skiff ride across Nymeve Lake to Darga's fortress is atmospheric: the moss-covered stone ruin retrofitted with gaudy tech and heavy weapons looms through the mist.

npcs.name: Warrick Raden
npcs.notes: Reluctant guide. Will present the heroes as entertainment.

passive.name: Fixer's Contacts
passive.description: Raden knows everyone in Darga's operation. Can identify guards, routines, and weak points.

actions.name: Spill Info
actions.description: Provide palace intel when motivated.

actions.name: Panic Shot
actions.description: Wild blaster shot when cornered. Low accuracy.

maneuver.name: Bolt
maneuver.description: Attempt to flee. Must be intercepted with Athletics or Evasion.

threatBuild.notes: Raden is not a fighter. Power 3 = (Wits 4-1)+0. His value is information, not combat. (Recurring — same stat block as earlier scene.)

scenes.hazards: Crossing Nymeve Lake by skiff. Darga's fortress visible through the mist.

decisions.consequence: Physical challenge. Must prove combat prowess in Darga's arena.
Social/mental challenge. High-stakes games in Darga's court.
Most flexible cover but requires convincing backstory.

narrativeLinks.note: The heroes' chosen cover determines their audition in Darga's throne room.

skillChecks.context: Building a convincing cover identity for the palace infiltration.
skillChecks.success: Air-tight cover. Raden vouches convincingly.
skillChecks.failure: Cover has gaps. Darga's people may ask uncomfortable questions.

skillChecks.context: Reassuring Raden during the skiff ride. He's getting cold feet.
skillChecks.success: Raden steadies. He'll play his part.
skillChecks.failure: Raden is visibly nervous. Darga's court may notice.

skillChecks.context: Reading the lake conditions during the skiff crossing.
skillChecks.success: Notice: the lake is patrolled by aquatic predators. Useful if escape requires the water route.
skillChecks.failure: Unremarkable crossing.

environmentMechanics.name: Nymeve Lake Crossing
environmentMechanics.effect: Open water. No cover. If attacked, swimming in Takodana's lake is dangerous — aquatic predators.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Stay on the skiff. Don't fall in.

rewards.narrative: Cover identities established. Entry to Darga's court secured.

pacing.openingBeat: Raden outlines the plan. The party chooses their cover.
pacing.risingAction: The skiff ride across the misty lake. The fortress looms.
pacing.climax: The fortress gates open. No turning back.
pacing.resolution: The heroes enter Darga's court under false pretenses.

parts.title: The Court of the Crime Lord

scenes.title: The Audition
scenes.subtitle: The Throne Room
scenes.readAloud:
The heroes are led through damp, torch-lit corridors that smell of mold and roasting meat. You emerge into Darga's Throne Room — a sensory assault of thick smoke, frantic jizz-wailer music, and a crowd of sycophants, thieves, and scavengers.

At the far end, resting on a massive reinforced hover-sled, sits Darga the Hutt. He is bloated, scarred, and flanked by a polished silver protocol droid. Darga barks in Huttese, his deep voice shaking the room. The droid translates:

"The Mighty Darga is bored with beggars. Prove you are worth his air."
scenes.gmNotes: Darga demands an immediate demonstration. The Muscle gets a fight against a bodyguard or beast. The Face gets a battle of wits. The Tech gets ordered to fix something on Darga's hover-sled with a blaster at their head. If heroes succeed, Darga welcomes them as 'guests' (prisoners with privileges). He then turns on Raden — ordering Gamorreans to drag him to the dungeons as punishment. Raden's screams serve as a warning of the price of failure.

npcs.name: Darga the Hutt
npcs.notes: Bloated, scarred, paranoid. Sits on an electrum-plated hover-sled. Never speaks Basic — uses TC-663.

passive.name: Absolute Authority
passive.description: All allies in Darga's court gain +1 to Resist social attacks. His presence is law.

actions.name: Command

scenes.title: The Long Con
scenes.subtitle: The Court
scenes.readAloud:
Over the next day cycle, you navigate the dangerous politics of Darga's court. Admiral Varth coaches you over encrypted comms, pointing out targets and reminding you to keep your eyes on the prize.

Three events unfold that reveal the true scale of what you've stumbled into...
scenes.gmNotes:
Three key events during the 'free roaming' phase:

1. THE IRON RING DELEGATION: Trandoshan Slavers drag chained Nazren prisoners into court, arguing about payment. Eavesdropping reveals Darga supplies slave labor for Project Leviathan.

2. THE IMPERIAL BUYER: Lady Sinde arrives — a severe logistics officer for Project Leviathan. Varth recognizes her voice. She's negotiating the next shipment.

3. THE DROID RACE: Darga announces a droid race to celebrate his deal. This is the heroes' best chance — while Darga is distracted gambling, they can slip away to slice the datapad or explore secure areas.

TC-663 can be turned if heroes treat him with respect or use Switch's code phrase — he'll provide palace maps and datapad location.

scenes.title: The Complication
scenes.subtitle: The Dungeon
scenes.readAloud:
Using the cover of the droid race, you slip into the restricted lower levels. Instead of a vault, you find a high-security detention block guarded by elite droids.

Inside the most secure cell is a stasis pod hooked to life-support machinery. Through the frost-covered viewport, you see a human woman in tattered Jedi robes, suspended in a Force-induced coma. Her eyes are closed, but you feel something — a whisper at the edge of your consciousness.

Admiral Varth's voice turns cold over the comms: "Leave her. She is a liability. If you wake a Jedi, every Inquisitor in the sector will hunt us down. Get the keys and get out."
scenes.gmNotes: This is the campaign's first major Moral Event Horizon. Jedi Master Denia was found by Darga after Order 66 and kept as a trophy to auction to the Inquisitorius. She's weak, barely alive, and telepathically reaching out to the heroes. Varth is furious — a Jedi is a heat magnet. This decision shapes the rest of the campaign. Saving Denia brings the Inquisitorius down on the heroes but gains them a powerful ally. Leaving her preserves the 'scoundrel' path but at a moral cost.

scenes.title: The Turn
scenes.subtitle: The Alarm
scenes.readAloud:
Klaxons scream through the stone corridors. The fortress goes into lockdown — blast doors grinding shut, red emergency lighting flooding every hall.

In the Throne Room above, Darga's booming voice shakes the walls:

"SPIES! They are Varth's ghosts! KILL THEM ALL!"

Darga's hover-sled engages its repulsors as he flees toward his private hangar bay, clutching the datapad with the encryption keys.
scenes.gmNotes: The alarm is triggered either by rescuing Denia (stasis disconnect + Demos sensing the Force) or by a failed datapad heist. The fortress is now hostile. Heroes in the dungeon are blocked by blast doors. Heroes in the court are surrounded by Gamorreans and Trandoshans. Darga is fleeing with the datapad. The heist has become a fight for survival and a race to catch the Hutt.

scenes.title: The Majordomo's Stand
scenes.readAloud:
Blocking your path stands Igren Demos, the Neimoidian Majordomo. He is no longer simpering. He holds a vibro-staff with lethal confidence, flanked by his elite Trandoshan bodyguards.

His red eyes widen as he senses the Jedi behind you. "The Jedi... she's awake. Give her to me. I will take her to the Inquisitorius myself and claim what Darga never could."
scenes.gmNotes: Demos is the boss fight of this location. He reveals his Force-sensitivity (untrained) and uses minor Force powers — Force Slam to separate heroes, Force Push to knock them into environmental hazards. His elite Trandoshan bodyguards provide additional threat. Varth screams over comms that Darga is getting away — Demos is stalling. The fight must be decisive.

scenes.title: The Hangar Bay
scenes.readAloud:
You burst through the blast doors into the humid, open-air hangar. You're too late — Darga's massive luxury yacht, the Glorious Chariot, is already hovering on repulsors, engines flaring blue-white. The boarding ramp retracts as the gold-and-purple hull begins to rise.

But it's not just Darga. Lady Sinde has ordered her Stormtrooper escort to secure the hangar and eliminate all witnesses.

Blaster fire erupts from behind crates of Tibanna gas as white-armored figures take firing positions.
scenes.gmNotes: Darga escapes in the Glorious Chariot, collapsing part of the ceiling to discourage pursuit. Lady Sinde's Stormtroopers are eliminating witnesses to protect Project Leviathan's secrecy. The heroes fight across the hangar using crates and equipment for cover. If fast enough, they can reach their ship and launch. They see Darga's yacht rocket toward the atmosphere and jump to hyperspace. Varth gets a partial trajectory lock — the vector aligns with the Anoat Sector. Bespin. Cloud City.

adventures.title: The Queen of Air and Darkness
adventures.summary: The heroes chase Darga the Hutt to Cloud City on Bespin, infiltrate a Sabacc tournament to intercept an Imperial payment, and face a desperate hangar bay showdown that ends with an orbital battle and a moral choice that defines the party.

gearNotes.reason: Varth coaches from the ship throughout — tournament strategy, Arandis identification, code decryption. Casino is crawling with Imperial undercover agents. Open comms = dead.
Commandeering the Glorious Chariot and Z-95s under fire with 30 seconds before a Stormtrooper platoon arrives. No time to improvise if something doesn't start cleanly.
Casino shootout into hangar battle into orbital combat — sustained multi-scene fighting with zero rest.
Catwalks over Bespin's atmosphere at Refinery Platform 13 and the open hangar bay. Safety line if someone gets knocked off — the drop is infinite.
Refinery recon ('they haven't seen you yet') and spotting Arandis's bodyguard positioning at the hotel.
Three weaponized dealer droids with hidden blaster cannons plus a heavy security droid at the hangar. Four droid encounters in one adventure.
Casino three-way firefight, city chase, and hangar assault — sightline control across three consecutive combat scenes.
Maintaining a convincing high-roller identity over a multi-day tournament. Also useful if Kessra from Adv2 shows up as hired muscle and might recognize a face.
Bugging Arandis's hotel conversations or his comms with the Stormtrooper detail.
Intercepting communications between Arandis and his handlers. Also useful during the chase to monitor Wing Guard frequencies.
Documenting Arandis's deliberate losing patterns as evidence of the Imperial payment scheme.
Sealed blast doors during the city chase if Varth's hack fails. Also cutting into the yacht if Darga seals the boarding ramp.
Hangar bay area denial against clustered pilots scrambling to Z-95 cockpits.
Casino surveillance avoidance when infiltrating as droid maintenance crew. Also useful during the Gauntlet chase.
Patching up between the casino firefight and the hangar assault. No time to rest.
Disabling the yacht's engines from outside if the heroes choose not to board.

scenes.title: The Brick Wall
scenes.readAloud:
Cloud City rises from the roiling orange clouds of Bespin like a crown of durasteel. In this era, it is not a polished resort — it is a rough-and-tumble mining boomtown, a Wild West in the clouds. Prospectors, gamblers, and fugitives pack the Cloudscape Platforms.

You hit the streets to find Darga — but the underworld is sealed tight. Every contact clams up. Then your datapads beep with an unsigned message:

"Stop digging. The drop is long, and the gas pressure is high. Go home."
scenes.gmNotes: Cloud City in 16 BBY is a mining boomtown, not Lando's resort. The heroes face a wall of silence — Darga has bribed the Wing Guard and port officials. The threatening message traces to the Parliament of Guilds (Ugnaught labor leadership), not Darga. Varth acts as 'The Man in the Chair,' analyzing data from the ship. The investigation leads to the Industrial District.

scenes.title: The Industrial Connection
scenes.readAloud:
You descend into The Works — the noisy, steam-filled industrial bowels of Cloud City. The Ugnaughts who sent the threat aren't enemies; they're terrified administrators named Fyren and Krygg.

They admit they were trying to scare you away to prevent a gang war. They hate Darga. The Hutt is forcing Ugnaught riggers to siphon military-grade gas directly to Imperial ships, bypassing Guild quotas.

"Help us stop his thugs from stealing a shipment right now, and we'll tell you exactly how the handover at the tournament works."
scenes.gmNotes: The Ugnaughts are potential allies. They offer a trade: stop Darga's thugs from stealing a gas shipment at Refinery Platform 13, and they'll reveal the tournament handover structure. This establishes the Ugnaught labor exploitation angle and gives the heroes a clear objective.

npcs.notes: Terrified but cooperative. Wants Darga gone.

scenes.title: The Refinery Rumble
scenes.readAloud:
Refinery Platform 13 hangs deep in the gas giant's atmosphere. The wind howls across exposed catwalks, and steam vents obscure your vision. Below, there is nothing — just the infinite amber depths of Bespin.

On the platform, Trandoshan Mercenaries and corrupt Ugnaught riggers are loading gas canisters onto a shuttle. They haven't seen you yet.
scenes.gmNotes: Tactical combat on catwalks over a lethal drop. Stray shots risk rupturing gas tanks (explosions) or steam pipes (scald damage). The objective is to defeat the Trandoshans and secure the platform. A datapad on the Trandoshan leader confirms the gas is destined for the Imperial Navy and mentions a ship called the 'Shackles of Nizon.'

scenes.title: The Imperial Mark
scenes.readAloud:
The Ugnaughts pay their debt. The handover isn't happening in secret — Darga insisted it occur in plain sight during the Cloud City Sabacc Tournament.

At the Yerith Bespin Hotel, you spot the Imperial Bagman: Lieutenant Armen Arandis. Dressed in civilian luxury but moving with military stiffness, flanked by 'bodyguards' who are clearly undercover Stormtroopers.

Varth's voice crackles in your ear: "That's the Bagman. He isn't here to play cards — he's here to transfer credits to Darga's proxy by losing intentionally. We need to be at that table."
scenes.gmNotes: Arandis is an Imperial math genius posing as a gambler. His job is to lose the pot intentionally to Darga's proxy. The heroes need to infiltrate the tournament to intercept the credits. The buy-in is massive (10,000 credits) — Varth provides the stake. Heroes must decide roles: The Player (gambler), The Security (hired guards), The Cheat (droid maintenance infiltrator).

npcs.notes: Mathematical genius. Cold, emotionless. Here to lose intentionally.

gambit.description: In Sabacc: force a specific card outcome. In combat: predict enemy movement for guaranteed hit.

threatBuild.notes: Operative. Power 5 = (Wits 5-1)+2. Disciplined: +1 Resist. Arandis is a genius pretending to be a gambler. His death triggers an alert to Draco.

scenes.title: The Buy-In
scenes.subtitle: Preparation
scenes.readAloud:
As you finalize your entry strategy, Varth's voice cuts in — tight with genuine fear.

"We have a problem. A massive energy spike just hit the system edge. An Imperial Star Destroyer just dropped out of hyperspace. It's the Assiduous — Inquisitor Draco's flagship."

A beat of silence.

"That ship is the clean-up crew. If Arandis loses the money wrong, or if Darga double-crosses them, Draco won't bother with arrests. He'll glass the city to bury the evidence. Win that pot and get the codes before Draco gets impatient."
scenes.gmNotes: The Assiduous arrives — escalating the stakes dramatically. Draco is the 'clean-up crew' for Project Leviathan. If the deal goes wrong, he'll destroy Cloud City to bury the evidence. This puts a hard clock on the entire tournament arc. The heroes must win the pot and get the gas codes before Draco loses patience.

npcs.name: Inquisitor Valin Draco
npcs.notes: Aboard the Assiduous in orbit. Prepared to 'glass the city' if needed.

passive.name: Dark Presence
passive.description: All enemies in the scene suffer -1 Power from fear unless they pass a Resolve check (T2/P0) at scene start.

actions.description: Crimson lightsaber attack. +2 Power. Ignores non-cortosis armor.

gambit.description: Area attack. All enemies in zone take damage and gain [Disoriented].

scenes.title: The Elimination Rounds
scenes.subtitle: Days 1 & 2
scenes.readAloud:
The Royal Casino is a loud, smoky, aggressive establishment where prospectors gamble away fortunes and refugees bet their last credits. Over fifty competitors crowd the tables. The air is thick with Corellian whiskey, Ryll spice smoke, and the mechanical whir of shuffling droids.

You notice two players worth watching: a flamboyant human named Silas Draver who seems drunk but isn't, and a cold Hapan noblewoman named Lady Fioro who bankrupts a weeping Dug in the first hour.
scenes.gmNotes:
Days 1-2 of the tournament. The heroes identify key players:
- Lt. Arandis at Table 4: plays like a machine. Folds strong hands, raises weak ones against specific opponents.
- Silas Draver: loud and arrogant but stone cold sober. Casing the room and checking camera sightlines. He's planning a heist.
- Lady Mirana Fioro: icy Hapan noblewoman. Keeps glancing at Arandis with calculation — she's Darga's proxy.

Varth coaches via comms: 'Watch who Arandis loses to. He's not allowed to lose to anyone but Darga's proxy.'

scenes.title: The Pressure Cooker
scenes.subtitle: Day 3
scenes.readAloud:
Day three. Only two tables remain. The buy-ins have escalated, and the audience has shifted from celebration to funeral silence.

Midway through the session, a Devaronian gambler named Szygar goes bust against Arandis. He snaps, leaping up and screaming accusations of cheating. A hold-out blaster appears in his hand.

Panic ripples through the crowd. Arandis doesn't flinch.
scenes.gmNotes: The Szygar incident is a test for the heroes. If working security, they must subdue him without causing a casino shutdown. If playing, they must decide whether to intervene. If heroes don't act, Arandis's undercover Stormtroopers will vaporize Szygar, potentially causing a police investigation that cancels the tournament. Varth: 'Don't let them shoot! If the cops shut us down, we lose the money!' Success earns trust from casino owner Jacc Mandelbrot.

scenes.title: The Final Table
scenes.subtitle: Day 4
scenes.readAloud:
The final day. Five players remain under a harsh spotlight: Arandis, Fioro, Draver, the Rodian Creeska, and you.

Arandis plays strangely — folding strong hands against everyone except Fioro. He goes All In against her. She calls.

He reveals a losing hand. The crowd erupts. But instead of sweeping his cards away, Arandis deliberately arranges them on the table: One of Coins. Three of Sabers. Seven of Flasks.

Varth decodes it instantly: "1-3-7. That's a frequency — 137.12. It's the slave circuit control code for the Shackles of Nizon. He just handed her the keys to the getaway car AND the money."
scenes.gmNotes: The climax of the game. Arandis deliberately loses the massive pot to Fioro (Darga's proxy), transferring the Imperial payment. The card arrangement is a code — the slave circuit frequency for the Shackles of Nizon. Fioro now has the credits (digitally transferred to a datapad) and the ship code. The heroes must grab her before she leaves. This is the moment the heist transitions to action.

scenes.title: The Heist
scenes.subtitle: Combat
scenes.readAloud:
Just as you move to intercept Fioro, Silas Draver makes his play. He stands, pulls a heavy blaster, and fires into the ceiling.

"Nobody moves! The Empire isn't the only one getting paid today!"

The dealer droids spin around, revealing hidden blaster cannons. Draver's men — disguised as waiters and guests — pull weapons and secure the exits. Draver grabs Fioro, pressing a blaster to her temple.

"The datapad. Now."
scenes.gmNotes: Three-way crossfire in a crowded casino. Draver weaponized the dealer droids and planted his men as staff. The heroes are caught between Draver's heist crew, panicking civilians, and Arandis who draws a concealed military blaster to protect the transaction's secrecy. Arandis likely dies in the crossfire, removing the Imperial witness. Varth: 'Forget the civilians! Get the datapad!' After defeating Draver, Fioro spills Darga's location: Docking Bay 4414.

scenes.title: The Aftermath
scenes.readAloud:
The casino is a wreck. Sirens wail in the distance. Lady Fioro, wounded and terrified, spills everything.

"He's at the lower docks — Docking Bay 4414. He has the Shackles of Nizon holding in orbit on slave circuits. He's taking the gas and the slaves and leaving the sector tonight!"

Varth scans the system. His voice is grim: "The Shackles just activated its engines. And... damn it. The Assiduous just entered the system. Draco is here. Get to the hangar. If Darga launches, we lose everything."
scenes.gmNotes: Fioro reveals Darga's location and the Shackles of Nizon's status. Varth confirms Draco has entered the system. The heroes must flee the crime scene and race through Cloud City to the industrial docks before Darga escapes. This is a transition scene — setting up Part 3.

scenes.title: The Gauntlet
scenes.subtitle: Streets of Cloud City
scenes.readAloud:
The journey to the lower docks is a sprint through a city in lockdown. Darga has bribed the Wing Guard to declare you 'Enemy Saboteurs.'

The chase transitions from pristine white corridors into the steam-filled, rusted catwalks of the Industrial District. Tibanna gas vents hiss, obscuring vision, and the orange glow of the Bespin sunset filters through heavy industrial grates.

Varth hacks door controls from the ship: "Left at the junction! No, the other left! I'm jamming their comms, but I can't stop them from shooting at you. Move!"
scenes.gmNotes: Running battle through Cloud City. The heroes are pinned down by corrupt Wing Guard and Stormtroopers. This is a chase — stopping to fight every enemy means being overwhelmed. Use the environment: shoot steam pipes to scald pursuers, jump onto moving cargo skiffs, cut through maintenance ducts. Varth acts as 'The Man in the Chair,' hacking door controls.

scenes.title: Hutt's Hangar
scenes.subtitle: Docking Bay 4414
scenes.readAloud:
You kick open the blast doors to Docking Bay 4414. The cavernous hangar is open to the roiling clouds of Bespin on one side, wind howling through the magnetic field.

Dominating the center is Darga's personal luxury yacht — the Glorious Chariot. Gold and purple, engines whining with a turbine squeal that vibrates in your teeth. Six Z-95 Headhunters idle on the deck, pilots scrambling into cockpits.

Darga stands at the base of the ramp, screaming at his loading crews. When he sees you, fear becomes rage.

"Kill them! They brought the Inquisitor down on us! I want their heads!"
scenes.gmNotes: The final confrontation with Darga. The Glorious Chariot is priming for launch. Six Z-95 fighters are scrambling — if they get airborne, they'll strafe the hangar. Savvy heroes might shoot pilots on ladders to hijack fighters. Darga retreats up the ramp as mercenaries fall. Heroes must breach the ship or disable its engines. If they board, they fight Darga in the yacht's narrow corridors where his massive bulk is a weapon.

scenes.title: The Firefight
scenes.readAloud:
The hangar erupts into chaos. Blaster bolts ricochet off hull plating and fuel crates. The Z-95 engines roar to life one by one. Darga, seeing his mercenaries fall, turns and lumbers up the boarding ramp of the Glorious Chariot.

The yacht's engines begin their warm-up cycle. If he seals that ramp, he's gone.
scenes.gmNotes: The climactic ground fight. Heroes must cross the hangar using fuel crates and machinery for cover. Primary objective: stop the Glorious Chariot from launching. If Z-95s get airborne, they strafe the hangar floor. If heroes breach the yacht, they fight Darga in opulent but narrow corridors. Darga fights desperately — he knows this is his last stand.

scenes.title: Grand Theft Starship
scenes.readAloud:
With Darga defeated and the hangar secured, you barely have time to breathe before Varth barks over the comms:

"Imperial stormtroopers have breached the elevator shaft! You have thirty seconds before a platoon comes through that door. You can't go back the way you came. Take the ships! Steal the yacht! I'll rendezvous with you in orbit!"
scenes.gmNotes: The heroes commandeer the Glorious Chariot and any intact Z-95 Headhunters. As hangar doors blast open and Stormtroopers pour in, the heroes punch the throttles and blast out of the hangar bay into the orange clouds of Bespin. This transitions to the orbital battle.

scenes.title: Skirmish Over Bespin
scenes.readAloud:
You break atmosphere and the sensor board lights up. The Imperial Star Destroyer Assiduous hangs in high orbit, launching TIE Fighters. The Shackles of Nizon — a transport filled with slaves — drifts dead in space. A massive automated Tibanna Gas Hauler is burning hard on a collision course with the slave ship.

Varth's voice is cold and professional:

"Good work getting airborne. That Gas Hauler is going to impact the transport in less than two minutes. It's a tragedy, but it's not our tragedy. We have the credits, we have the data. Spool up the hyperdrives. Let's get out of here while we're rich."

Then a frantic transmission cuts through:

"Varth! I'm on the transport! The slave circuits are locked! I can't steer and sensors show collision imminent! Where is that backup code?!"

It's Captain Okeefe.
scenes.gmNotes: The moral climax of Act I. Varth sent Okeefe to the slave ship intending for her to be destroyed with the evidence — she's a loose end. The Empire is using the Gas Hauler as a ram to destroy the Shackles of Nizon. Varth's position: 'She knew the risks. We jump. Now.' The heroes must choose: follow Varth and leave Okeefe to die, or turn and fight. If they fight, they engage TIE Fighters to destroy the Gas Hauler. Varth eventually helps — not from altruism, but because he needs the heroes alive for the fortune.

pacing.openingBeat: Break atmosphere. Sensors light up. TIEs, Star Destroyer, and a collision countdown.

pacing.resolution: Explosion. Okeefe's voice: "Slave circuits fried. We're jumping."

scenes.title: The Choice
scenes.subtitle: End of Act I
scenes.readAloud:
The Tibanna Gas Hauler explodes in a massive fireball just meters from the slave ship. Okeefe's voice, shaking with relief: "Slave circuits fried. Nav-computer is green. We are jumping to the rendezvous..."

A pause.

"...and Varth? You and I are going to have a very long talk."

The Assiduous opens fire with turbolasers. Green bolts flash past your cockpit. Varth screams:

"We overstayed our welcome! PUNCH IT!"

The stars stretch into lines and you're gone.
scenes.gmNotes: The aftermath of Act I. At the rendezvous: Okeefe punches Varth in the jaw. He takes it, claiming it was a 'tactical calculation that changed.' Rewards: The Glorious Chariot, Z-95 fighters, tournament winnings, Darga's assets, Project Leviathan files. Varth's tease: 'This data points to a massive construction project in the Unknown Regions. If you want to know what the Empire is really building, we have to go off the map.' This concludes Act I and sets up the campaign's second act.
